The output from the encoder is projected using a linear layer and [MASK] tokens with position embeddings are added to generate the full set of tokens corresponding to the input frame.
We explore three different decoder configurations which operate on the full set of tokens.
A joint decoder applies vanilla Transformer blocks on the concatenation of full set of tokens from both frames.
A cross-self decoder is similar to the original encoder-decoder design of the Transformer model. Each decoder block consists of a cross-attention layer and a self-attention layer.
The cross-attention layer is functionally similar to the affinity matrix often used in self-supervised correspondence learning approaches.
A cross decoder consists of decoder blocks with only cross-attention layers, where tokens from f2 attend to the tokens from f1.
